2605.04496.title: SCOUT: Active Information Foraging for Long-Text Understanding with Decoupled Epistemic States
2605.04496.paper_summary: Long-Text Understanding (LTU) at million-token scale requires balancing reasoning fidelity with computational efficiency. Frontier long-context LLMs can process millions of token contexts end-to-end, but they suffer from high token consumption and attention dilution. In parallel, specialized LTU agents often sacrifice fidelity through task-agnostic abstractions like graph construction or indexing. We identify a key insight for LTU: query-relevant information is typically sparse relative to the full document, so effective reasoning should rely on a query-sufficient subset rather than the entire context. To address this, we propose SCOUT, a new paradigm for LTU that shifts from passive processing to active information foraging. It treats the document as an explorable environment and answers from a compact, provenance-grounded epistemic state. Guided by state-level gap diagnosis, SCOUT adaptively alternates between coarse-to-fine exploration and anchored state updates that progressively contract its epistemic state toward query sufficiency. Experiments show that SCOUT matches state-of-the-art proprietary models while reducing token consumption by up to 8x. Moreover, SCOUT remains stable as context length scales, substantially alleviating the practical cost-performance trade-off.
2605.04496.paper_summary_zh: 長文本理解 (LTU) 在百萬標記的規模上需要平衡推理的準確性與計算效率。前沿的長上下文 LLM 可以端到端處理數百萬的標記上下文，但它們面臨著高標記消耗和注意力稀釋的問題。與此同時，專門的 LTU 代理通常通過任務無關的抽象，如圖形構建或索引，來犧牲準確性。我們確定了 LTU 的一個關鍵見解：相對於整個文檔，查詢相關的信息通常是稀疏的，因此有效的推理應該依賴於一個足夠的查詢子集，而不是整個上下文。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 SCOUT，一種新的 LTU 範式，從被動處理轉向主動的信息覓取。它將文檔視為一個可探索的環境，並從一個緊湊的、基於來源的認識狀態中回答問題。在狀態級別的差距診斷指導下，SCOUT 自適應地在粗到細的探索和固定狀態更新之間交替，逐步收縮其認識狀態以達到查詢的充分性。實驗表明，SCOUT 的性能與最先進的專有模型相當，同時將標記消耗降低了多達 8 倍。此外，隨著上下文長度的擴展，SCOUT 仍然保持穩定，顯著減輕了實際成本與性能之間的權衡。
2605.04496.author: Zhenliang Zhang et.al.
2605.04496.authors: Zhenliang Zhang, Wenqing Wang, Yong Hu, Yaming Yang, Jiaheng Gao, Chen Shen, Xiaojun Wan
